_meta.name: NREL-118 headline emission-reduction targets
_meta.description: Regression targets for the gtopt port of the NREL-118 test system (Pena/Martinez-Anido/Hodge 2018). Derived from the paper abstract and the single direct-quote snippet (source 10) we could recover without paywall access. Future-work: split into per-scenario rows (5, 10, ..., 30, 33 % wind+solar) once a paper PDF is available institutionally.

source_paper.title: An Extended IEEE 118-Bus Test System With High Renewable Penetration

_meta.evidence_quote: up to 33% wind and solar energy penetration in the United States' portion of the Western grid avoids 29%-34% carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions
_meta.evidence_quote_source: Google-indexed snippet from a SciSpace listing of the paper (source 10 in PROVENANCE.md). The CO2 figure also appears in Hodge group public-talk slides and is widely cited.
_meta.scenario_definition: 33% wind+solar penetration vs the no-renewables baseline, full-year (8784 h) production-cost simulation

regression_window_for_single_block_test.rationale: single-block deterministic LP is noisier than 8784-h UC; widen by ~10 pp on each side

nox_reduction_pct_at_33pct_renewables.note: Not enforced yet — gtopt fuel model does not emit per-pollutant NOx; reserved for the future multi-pollutant feature.

so2_reduction_pct_at_33pct_renewables.note: Not enforced yet — gtopt fuel model does not emit per-pollutant SO2; reserved for the future multi-pollutant feature.

ipcc_combustion_factors_used_by_converte._source: IPCC AR6 WG3 Annex III Table A.III.2, mirrored in scripts/nrel118_to_gtopt/_converter.py and test/source/test_emission_nrel118_port.cpp
ipcc_combustion_factors_used_by_converte._units: tCO2 per GJ of fuel energy
